_note: Synthetic, fictional sample member for the take-home. No real person or PHI. Use only as seed data.

goals.text: Optimize testosterone and cortisol levels through training stimulus
Build a sustainable training base for long-term health and longevity
Complete HYROX race in under 70 minutes
Resolve desk-induced lumbar pain and restore full hip/T-spine mobility

preferences.notes: Former gymnast, loves calisthenics and gymnastics-derived movements. Full gym access including rig and rings. Prefers compound movements and functional patterns. Desk-bound founder — morning sessions only.

injuries.diagnosis: Mechanical low-back pain — desk-induced. Reduced hip flexor length and T-spine mobility contributing to lumbar overload under flexion and compressive load. No disc pathology on imaging.
injuries.notes: Onset correlated with extended remote work periods (10+ hrs/day seated). Pain on lumbar flexion under load and sustained compressive loading (heavy deadlifts, good mornings). Responds well to extension-biased work and hip mobility drills.

states.notes: Post-long-flight stiffness. Lumbar tight and painful on forward bend under any load.
Better after mobility work yesterday. Still painful on loaded flexion — avoiding deadlifts and good mornings.

chat_history.text: Crushed the calisthenics session. Rings felt great. Back held up well on the rows.
Excellent. Your ring strength is at an elite level. Let's keep the lumbar load conservative this week and prioritize hip mobility before we reintroduce barbell hip hinges.
Good session today. SkiErg intervals are brutal but I can feel my conditioning improving. Back was a 3/10 during carries — manageable.
Just got back from SF. 5-hour flight left my back stiff as a board. Pain is a 4 today. Going to do the mobility protocol.

morning_tasks.text: Confirm Mico's lumbar status today — last check-in was 3/10 pain with mild inflammation. Avoid loaded lumbar flexion in today's session.
Today is a good day for upper-body calisthenics or conditioning. Hip hinge reintroduction (barbell RDL) can begin next week if lumbar pain drops below 2/10.
Mico hit 100% adherence for 4 consecutive weeks — acknowledge the consistency.

reasons: High engagement in chat — daily check-ins
Strong progress on calisthenics goals (ring strength, handstand)
